posts.title: How Often Should You Really Dry Clean Your Suit?
posts.excerpt: The honest answer surprises most professionals. Here's the rule fabric specialists actually follow — and why over-cleaning ruins your wardrobe.
posts.author: Aarav Mehta
posts.content:
Walk into any tailor shop in Mumbai or Delhi and ask how often you should dry clean a wool suit, and you'll get five different answers. The truth, according to garment-care chemists, sits somewhere between common sense and chemistry.

The 4-wear rule

For a standard wool or wool-blend suit worn in Indian climates, dry cleaning every 3-4 wears is ideal. Cleaning more often degrades fibres; cleaning less often allows oils, sweat and dust to settle permanently into the weave.

What to do between dry cleans

  • Brush the suit with a soft horsehair brush after each wear
  • Hang on a wide wooden hanger for 24 hours before re-wearing
  • Spot-treat small stains immediately — never let them set
  • Steam, don't iron, to refresh the fabric

When to dry clean immediately

Visible stains, monsoon exposure, smoke or food odours, or before long-term storage — these are non-negotiable. A suit packed away dirty will not survive a season.

At Lachman Sons Drycleaners we use a low-temperature hydrocarbon process that's gentler on natural fibres than the older perc-based systems still common in India. The result: your suit lasts twice as long.

posts.title: Saving a Stained Silk Saree: A Step-by-Step Guide
posts.excerpt: Turmeric, oil, paan, wine — silk doesn't forgive. But with the right first response, even heirloom sarees can be rescued.
posts.author: Priya Sharma
posts.content:
A silk saree is rarely just a garment. It carries weddings, festivals, sometimes generations. Which is exactly why a single stain feels catastrophic.

The first 60 seconds matter most

Blot, never rub. Use a clean white cotton cloth and press gently from the outside of the stain inward. Rubbing pushes the stain deeper into the fibres and can permanently dull the silk's sheen.

Stain-by-stain triage

  • Turmeric — Do not apply water immediately. Sprinkle talcum powder, let it absorb the oil, then bring to us within 24 hours.
  • Oil / Ghee — Cornflour or talc to absorb. Never hot water.
  • Paan / Wine — Cold water blot only. These oxidise quickly; speed matters.
  • Ink — Stop. Don't try home remedies. Bring it to us.

What we do differently

Silk requires solvent-based cleaning, never water-based. Our specialists test each saree on an inner seam first, identify the dye type, and use targeted solvents. Heirloom sarees go through a separate hand-care line.

Bring it within 48 hours and your odds jump from coin-flip to near certainty.

posts.title: The Truth About Eco-Friendly Dry Cleaning
posts.excerpt: "Green" is the most over-used word in our industry. Here's how to tell a genuinely eco process from a marketing label.
posts.author: Rohan Kapoor
posts.content:
Every dry cleaner in India now claims to be "eco-friendly." Few actually are. Here's the chemistry behind the claim.

What dry cleaning actually uses

Traditional dry cleaning uses perchloroethylene (perc) — effective, cheap, and a recognised health and environmental hazard. Most cities now restrict it. The alternatives:

  • Hydrocarbon (DF-2000) — Petroleum-based, far gentler than perc, but still a fossil derivative.
  • Liquid CO₂ — Genuinely clean, but expensive and rare in India.
  • Wet cleaning — Water plus advanced detergents and computer-controlled tumblers. Excellent for many fabrics; not for everything.
  • SolvonK4 — Bio-based, biodegradable, modern. What we use.

Questions to ask any dry cleaner

  1. What solvent do you use? (If they can't name it, walk away.)
  2. What's the disposal process for used solvent?
  3. How often is the solvent filtered or distilled?

A real eco operation will answer all three immediately. "Green" without specifics is just paint.

posts.title: Monsoon Storage Checklist for Woollens
posts.excerpt: Indian monsoon humidity is the silent killer of winter wardrobes. A 7-step ritual that prevents mildew, moths, and yellowing.
posts.author: Ananya Iyer
posts.content:
By April every year we get the same panicked calls — sweaters pulled out smelling musty, suits with brown spots, shawls eaten through. All of it preventable with one Sunday afternoon of work.

The 7-step monsoon storage ritual

  1. Dry clean before storing — never store dirty woollens. Skin oils attract moths.
  2. Cool, dry location — top shelf of an interior cupboard, never near an outer wall.
  3. Cotton garment bags — never plastic. Plastic traps moisture.
  4. Cedar blocks or neem leaves — natural moth repellents. Replace every 2 months.
  5. Silica gel sachets — at least two per shelf, replaced monthly through monsoon.
  6. Air every 6 weeks — open the cupboard, take garments out, let them breathe for an hour.
  7. Inspect in October — before re-wearing, check for any signs of damage and re-clean if needed.

Or — drop them with us. We offer climate-controlled garment storage from June to October, individually packed and inspected monthly. One less thing to worry about.

posts.title: Behind the Scenes at Our Cleaning Plant
posts.excerpt: Take a tour of what actually happens to your clothes after pickup — and why the 19-step process makes a visible difference.
posts.author: LS Editorial
posts.content:
Most customers never see what happens between drop-off and delivery. Here's the full 19-step journey.

1. Tagging

Every garment gets an RFID tag at intake — not a paper slip. The tag carries your customer ID, fabric notes, and special instructions through every station.

2. Inspection

Pre-existing damage, missing buttons, weak seams — all photographed and logged. You receive a notification if anything needs repair before cleaning.

3. Stain mapping

Each stain is identified, classified by type (protein, oil, tannin, dye), and pre-treated with the appropriate agent.

4-12. Cleaning

Garments are sorted by colour, fabric, and treatment type. They flow through dedicated lines — silks never touch the same machine as denim.

13-17. Finishing

Steam pressing on form-fit mannequins, hand-pressing for delicate areas, button replacement, lint removal, final inspection under daylight-balanced lighting.

18-19. Packaging and delivery

Each piece is individually wrapped, hung on a custom hanger, and placed in a reusable garment bag. Driver delivers within your 30-minute window.

You won't see most of this. You will see the difference.
